public.aboutSubtitle: A secure portal for vendor onboarding, invoice submission, and invoice processing workflows within Indore Municipal Corporation.
public.purposeText: This portal standardizes invoice handling across IMC and its vendors, improves traceability, and enables role-based scrutiny and approvals.
public.whoVendor: Vendors registered with IMC for uploading invoices and tracking status.
public.whoOfficials: IMC Officials with assigned roles for review, verification, approval, and reporting.
public.included4: Invoice status tracking with audit trail.
public.included5: Helpdesk support for issue reporting (after login).
public.scopeNotesText: This portal is intended for invoice processing and related compliance checks. Payment execution timelines and banking operations follow IMC finance procedures and may occur outside the portal.
public.contactSubtitle: Use the helpdesk for ticket-based support and clear escalation. Keep invoice references handy when raising a request.
public.primaryChannelText: After login, submit a ticket through the in-portal helpdesk so your request is traceable.
public.includeLine: Include: Vendor ID, Invoice ID, Tender/Work Order reference, and screenshots (if applicable).
public.esc2: Level 2: Department/section follow-up referencing your ticket number.
public.esc3: Level 3: Formal escalation as per IMC internal procedures.
public.contactNote: Note: Update this page with official email/phone contacts once finalized for your deployment.
public.ticket1: Unable to login / role access issues.
public.ticket2: Invoice upload errors or document validation.
public.ticket4: Mismatch in invoice totals, GST details, or bank information.
public.guidelinesSubtitle: Basic submission rules and document requirements for vendors. Specific tender/work-order terms always take priority.
public.doc1: Signed invoice (as applicable) with correct invoice number and date.
public.doc2: Work order / contract / tender reference details.
public.doc3: GST details and supporting certificates (where applicable).
public.doc4: PAN and bank details for payment processing.
public.doc5: Completion certificates / measurement books / delivery challans (as applicable).
public.rule1: Enter invoice amount and tax details exactly as per the uploaded invoice document.
public.rule2: Ensure vendor name, GSTIN, and bank details match your registered profile.
public.rule3: Submit one invoice per unique invoice number; duplicates may be rejected.
public.rule4: Upload clear, readable documents; blurred or incomplete scans may be returned for correction.
public.fileTypesText: Upload formats depend on portal configuration. Prefer PDF for invoices. If images are allowed, use high-resolution scans.
public.stPending: Pending: Under scrutiny/review at an IMC processing stage.
public.stReturned: Returned for correction: Action required from vendor (or creator) to fix issues and resubmit.
public.stRejected: Rejected: Invoice is not acceptable as submitted; review comments and raise a helpdesk ticket if needed.
public.stApproved: Approved: Approved for next finance/payment procedure as per IMC process.
public.complianceText: Submissions must comply with applicable laws, IMC financial rules, and the terms of your work order/contract. Misrepresentation or incorrect documentation may lead to rejection and further action as per policy.
public.faqSubtitle: Quick answers for common vendor queries about invoices, documents, and status tracking.
public.basicLegalNote: This is a basic policy intended for public portals and should be reviewed by IMC legal/compliance.
public.basicTermsNote: This is a basic terms page and should be reviewed by IMC legal/compliance for production deployment.

privacy.infoCollect1: Account and profile details (vendor identity, contact details, bank details).
privacy.infoCollect2: Invoice data (invoice numbers, amounts, dates, references).
privacy.infoCollect3: Uploaded documents supporting invoice processing and compliance.
privacy.infoCollect4: Usage/security logs (login events, access records, audit trails).
privacy.use1: To onboard vendors and maintain verified vendor records.
privacy.use2: To process invoices through scrutiny, verification, and approval workflows.
privacy.use3: To communicate portal notifications and support responses.
privacy.use4: To detect and prevent fraud, abuse, or unauthorized access.
privacy.sharingText: Data may be shared with authorized IMC departments and service providers strictly for operating the portal, compliance, audits, and statutory requirements. Disclosure may occur when required by law or court orders.
privacy.cookiesText: The portal may use cookies or similar technologies to maintain login sessions and security controls. Disabling cookies may prevent you from using protected features.
privacy.retentionText: Information is retained as required for invoice processing, audits, and legal compliance. Reasonable technical and organizational measures are applied to protect data, but no system can be guaranteed 100% secure.
privacy.contactText: For privacy-related requests, use the portal helpdesk after login or refer to the Contact & Support page for escalation.

terms.acceptText: By accessing or using this portal, you agree to comply with these terms and all applicable laws and IMC procedures.
terms.respons1: Provide accurate information and maintain updated profile details.
terms.respons2: Upload authentic documents and correct invoice information.
terms.respons3: Keep credentials confidential and report suspected compromise promptly.
terms.respons4: Use the portal only for lawful and authorized purposes.
terms.rightsText: IMC may suspend or restrict access for security, compliance, maintenance, or suspected misuse. Actions may be taken as per applicable policies and legal requirements.
terms.availabilityText: The portal is provided on an “as available” basis. IMC may modify, suspend, or discontinue features with or without notice for operational reasons.
terms.liabilityText: To the extent permitted by law, IMC is not liable for indirect or consequential losses arising from use of the portal. This does not limit any rights that cannot be excluded under applicable law.
terms.governingText: Use of the portal is governed by applicable Indian laws and IMC procedures, and disputes are subject to jurisdiction as per law.

faq.q1: What file type should I upload for the invoice?
faq.a1: Prefer a clear PDF of the signed invoice. If image uploads are enabled, use high-resolution scans and ensure all pages are readable.
faq.q2: What should be included in the invoice format?
faq.a2: Invoice number & date, vendor name and address, GSTIN (if applicable), work order/tender reference, item/service details, taxable value, taxes, and final payable amount.
faq.q3: Why was my invoice “Returned for correction”?
faq.a3: Usually because a document is missing, values don’t match the uploaded invoice, or details like GST/bank info need correction. Check the remarks and resubmit after fixing.
faq.a4: Your invoice is in the IMC processing workflow (scrutiny/verification/approval). You can track status changes in the portal.
faq.a5: The invoice was not accepted as submitted. Review the rejection remarks. If you believe it is incorrect, raise a helpdesk ticket with the invoice reference.
faq.a6: Login and use the in-portal Helpdesk for ticket-based support and escalation. Keep your Vendor ID and Invoice ID ready.
